commands.review_summary:
The PR changes three usage-bar translator membership checks from prototype-chain `in` lookups to own-property `Object.hasOwn` lookups.

PR surface: Source 0. Total 0 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current `origin/main` checks user-authored alias and case tables with `in`, so values like `toString` or `constructor` can hit inherited Object prototype names instead of an authored entry or `_default`. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The maintainer action is to ask for contributor-supplied real behavior proof; automation could add a test, but it cannot prove the contributor's after-fix setup for this external PR.

target.title: fix: use Object.hasOwn instead of in operator in usage bar translator
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where the `in` operator in the usage bar translator would match inherited prototype properties like `toString`, `valueOf`, or `constructor` on vocabulary alias tables and segment case tables, potentially returning incorrect translations.

## Why This Change Was Made

Replace `key in table` with `Object.hasOwn(table, key)` in two locations (alias vocabulary lookup and segment case table lookup).

## User Impact

Fixes potential incorrect rendering of usage bar segments when lookup keys coincide with `Object.prototype` names.

## Evidence

- **`src/auto-reply/usage-bar/translator.test.ts`: 13/13 passed** ✅
- `Object.hasOwn` (ES2022) checks only own properties, unlike `in` which traverses the prototype chain
target.author: zhanglei99586
